subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, OHCHR, ICJ, Freedom House, RSF) + international press.
A genuine post-Jammeh democratic transition with active transitional justice - the TRRC's 16-volume report and the first-ever ECOWAS hybrid Special Tribunal (2024) to try Jammeh-era atrocities.

Standout features: a real democratic transition and recovery (~6% growth, single-digit inflation, falling debt under an IMF ECF/RSF), against backslide risk concentrated in Barrow's third-term bid for the Dec 2026 vote, weak healthcare, and flood exposure.

economic.notes: A recovering, IMF-programmed economy with falling debt and single-digit inflation, on a tiny, enclave-dependent base.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt falling to ~76% of GDP under an on-track IMF ECF, but still at high distress risk on a record nominal stock. (IMF/World Bank DSA Dec-2025)

monetary_independence.notes: An independent managed float of the dalasi with modest credibility; a thin FX market limits effectiveness. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dalasi is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Decelerated to ~7% by late 2025, back in single digits after a post-COVID spike. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A small system, broadly stable under IMF oversight; thin data and shallow markets. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A tiny open economy reliant on re-export trade, tourism (~20% of GDP), and remittances (~a third of GDP), enclaved within Senegal. (World Bank; IMF)

institutional.notes: A markedly improved rule-of-law trajectory with a credible transitional-justice track, on a thin base.

rule_of_law.notes: Markedly improved since 2017 with a credible transitional-justice track, tempered by weak capacity and corruption complaints. (Freedom House 2025; ICTJ)

statistical_integrity.notes: Engaged with IMF/World Bank through frequent reviews, with limited small-administration capacity. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A thin, under-resourced administration heavily reliant on donor support. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: 'Partly Free' - RSF ~10th in Africa (2025) but slipping, with 'bad media laws' cited. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Two peaceful elections and restored liberties, with a third-term bid as the looming stress test.

political_stability.notes: Two peaceful elections since 2016, but Barrow's third-term bid and failed term-limit reform make the Dec-2026 vote a democratic stress test. (Africa Center; Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Substantially restored post-Jammeh (assembly, speech), though LGBT+ discrimination and GBV persist. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systematic abuse of foreigners; tourism-friendly, with regional migration the main pressure. (observed)

social_cohesion.notes: A generally cohesive small society - ethnic politics exist but no organized violence. (Freedom House)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing - a near-term dividend but a long-term jobs strain driving out-migration. (UN/World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A stable ECOWAS member enmeshed with Senegal, conflict-free and unsanctioned.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable ECOWAS member deeply enmeshed with Senegal (which enforced the 2017 transition) and Western/EU-aligned. (ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict, sitting south of the Sahel insurgency belt and enclaved within stable Senegal. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Unsanctioned, an open IMF-program economy, with thin FX reserves a structural watch-item. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Improving but weak infrastructure and healthcare, with coastal/river flood exposure.

climate.notes: A low-lying Atlantic delta state - acute sea-level/saline-intrusion plus coastal erosion, tiny adaptive capacity. (ND-GAIN; IPCC)

healthcare.notes: A weak, donor-dependent system off a low baseline. (World Bank/WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (electricity restoration, a 2025 climate-resilient transport project) but power and rural roads remain weak. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Relatively safe by regional standards - petty crime in tourist areas, low violent crime. (OSAC)

summary: A small, poor, positively-skewed recovery story: a managed-float dalasi economy of tourism (~20% of GDP), remittances (~a third of GDP), and agriculture, enclaved within Senegal, growing ~6% with single-digit inflation and falling debt under an on-track IMF ECF/RSF program. Its standout asset is a real democratic transition with active transitional justice - the TRRC's 16-volume report and the first-ever ECOWAS hybrid Special Tribunal to try Jammeh-era atrocities. The principal risk is concentrated and political: President Barrow abandoned his single-term pledge, blocked term-limit reform, and is seeking a third term in the December 2026 election. Physical fundamentals (weak healthcare, flood exposure, thin infrastructure) remain poor, but the absence of conflict and the improving institutional trajectory keep the overall shape favorable.
